demo_timestamps.description: Demonstration of a workflow solution using Revit, JSON files, and custom AI tools to compare design variants and calculate carbon emissions.
Demonstrating the process of importing carbon data into Revit, using the Viewer SDK to visualize and interact with the carbon data.

themes: Challenges in Software Development and Data Quality

client_mentions.name: Swiss
client_mentions.context: Greg Wachelschel and Lawrence Glutte are part of Swiss BIM team presenting their project.

client_mentions.name: Suez
client_mentions.context: Development and hosting of the custom GPT tool internally.

ai_technologies.name: GPT
ai_technologies.context: Decarbon Exo-GPT used to process JSON files and automate carbon data extraction and structuring.

ai_technologies.name: QSAMGPTs
ai_technologies.context: Used for supporting low-coding approaches in software development.

summary: The video showcases a project by a Swiss BIM team that develops a carbon scenario comparison workflow utilizing Autodesk's APS and APIs to automate and optimize carbon emissions analysis in design scenarios. It highlights the integration of carbon data into BIM models using Autodesk Revit and the Viewer SDK, addressing challenges like data quality and software limitations while planning to enhance automation and improve the carbon calculator. The project aims to make carbon impacts visible and actionable, fostering sustainable infrastructure design and improving workflow efficiency and scalability.
